docs.title: Django N+1
docs.text:
N+1 query detection for Django.

Detects N+1 queries (lazy loading related objects in a loop) and unused eager loads (select_related/prefetch_related that are never accessed) in your Django application.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• N+1 detection — Warns when a related object is lazily loaded on an instance that was part of a bulk query
  • Unused eager load detection — Warns when select_related or prefetch_related results are never accessed
  • Middleware — Automatically monitors all requests
  • pytest plugin — nplus1 fixture and @pytest.mark.nplus1 marker for test-time detection
  • Profiler — Context manager for manual use in scripts or tests
  • Whitelisting — Ignore specific model/field combinations with wildcard support
  • Zero runtime dependencies — Only requires Django

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install django-nplus1

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    ...,
    "django_nplus1",
]

MIDDLEWARE = [
    ...,
    "django_nplus1.NPlusOneMiddleware",
]

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.12+
  • Django 5.2+

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.12+
  • Django 5.2+

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install django-nplus1

Or with uv:

uv add django-nplus1

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Add django_nplus1 to your INSTALLED_APPS and the middleware to MIDDLEWARE:

# settings.py
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    ...,
    "django_nplus1",
]

MIDDLEWARE = [
    ...,
    "django_nplus1.NPlusOneMiddleware",  # should be last
]

The middleware will now log warnings when N+1 queries or unused eager loads are detected.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text: After installation, the middleware will automatically detect N+1 queries during request handling and log warnings.

docs.title: Example N+1 Query
docs.text:
# This triggers a warning:
users = list(User.objects.all())
for user in users:
    print(user.profile)  # N+1! Each access triggers a separate query

docs.title: Fix with select_related
docs.text:
# No warning:
users = list(User.objects.select_related("profile").all())
for user in users:
    print(user.profile)  # Already loaded

docs.title: Raise Exceptions in Tests
docs.text:
# settings/test.py
NPLUS1_RAISE = True  # Raise NPlusOneError instead of logging

docs.title: Using the pytest Plugin
docs.text:
# Automatic detection with marker
@pytest.mark.nplus1
def test_my_view(client):
    client.get("/my-view/")  # Fails if N+1 detected

# Manual detection with fixture
def test_manual(nplus1):
    users = list(User.objects.all())
    users[0].profile  # Raises NPlusOneError

docs.title: Using the Profiler
docs.text:
from django_nplus1 import Profiler

with Profiler():
    users = list(User.objects.all())
    users[0].profile  # Raises NPlusOneError

docs.title: NPlusOneMiddleware
docs.text:
Django middleware that detects N+1 queries and unused eager loads during request processing.

MIDDLEWARE = [
    ...,
    "django_nplus1.NPlusOneMiddleware",
]

docs.title: Profiler
docs.text:
Context manager for manual N+1 detection.

from django_nplus1 import Profiler

with Profiler(whitelist=None) as p:
    ...  # Any N+1 queries here raise NPlusOneError

Parameters:

  • whitelist (optional): List of dicts with model, field, and/or label keys.

docs.title: NPlusOneError
docs.text:
Raised when an N+1 query or unused eager load is detected (when NPLUS1_RAISE=True or using Profiler).

from django_nplus1.exceptions import NPlusOneError

docs.title: Fixtures
docs.text: • nplus1 — Yields a Profiler instance. Test fails on N+1 detection.

docs.title: Markers
docs.text:
• @pytest.mark.nplus1 — Auto-detect N+1 in the marked test.
  • @pytest.mark.nplus1(whitelist=[...]) — With whitelisting.

Initial release.

  • N+1 lazy load detection
  • Unused eager load detection (select_related / prefetch_related)
  • Django middleware
  • pytest plugin with nplus1 fixture and @pytest.mark.nplus1 marker
  • Profiler context manager
  • Whitelisting with wildcard support
  • Python 3.12+ / Django 5.2+ support

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All settings are optional and configured in your Django settings module.

docs.title: NPLUS1_LOG
docs.text:
Enable/disable logging of detected issues. Default: True.

NPLUS1_LOG = True

docs.title: NPLUS1_LOG_LEVEL
docs.text:
Python logging level for detected issues. Default: logging.WARNING.

import logging
NPLUS1_LOG_LEVEL = logging.WARNING

docs.title: NPLUS1_LOGGER
docs.text:
Custom logger instance. Default: logging.getLogger("django_nplus1").

import logging
NPLUS1_LOGGER = logging.getLogger("my_app.nplus1")

docs.title: NPLUS1_RAISE
docs.text:
Raise NPlusOneError on detection instead of (or in addition to) logging. Default: False.

NPLUS1_RAISE = True  # Recommended for test settings

docs.title: NPLUS1_ERROR
docs.text:
Custom exception class to raise. Default: NPlusOneError.

NPLUS1_ERROR = MyCustomError

docs.title: NPLUS1_WHITELIST
docs.text:
List of patterns to ignore. See Whitelisting for details.

NPLUS1_WHITELIST = [
    {"model": "myapp.User", "field": "profile"},
    {"model": "auth.*"},
]

docs.title: Recommended Test Configuration
docs.text:
# settings/test.py
NPLUS1_RAISE = True
NPLUS1_WHITELIST = [
    # Known acceptable patterns
]

docs.title: pytest Plugin
docs.text: django-nplus1 includes a pytest plugin that is automatically discovered via entry points.

docs.title: nplus1
docs.text:
A fixture that provides a Profiler context. Any N+1 queries detected within the test will raise NPlusOneError.

def test_my_view(nplus1, client):
    client.get("/my-view/")  # Raises if N+1 detected

docs.title: @pytest.mark.nplus1
docs.text:
Mark a test for automatic N+1 detection. The entire test is wrapped in a Profiler.

@pytest.mark.nplus1
def test_my_view(client):
    client.get("/my-view/")

With whitelisting:

@pytest.mark.nplus1(whitelist=[{"model": "auth.User"}])
def test_with_whitelist(client):
    client.get("/my-view/")

docs.title: Disabling the Plugin
docs.text:
If you need to disable the plugin for specific tests, you can use the -p flag:

pytest -p no:nplus1

docs.title: Whitelisting
docs.text: Whitelist specific model/field combinations to suppress warnings for known acceptable patterns.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
NPLUS1_WHITELIST = [
    {"model": "myapp.User", "field": "profile"},
    {"model": "auth.*"},                          # Wildcard model
    {"label": "n_plus_one", "model": "myapp.Post"},  # Only N+1, not unused eager
]

docs.title: Pattern Options
docs.text:
Each whitelist entry is a dictionary with optional keys:

Key Description Example model Model class or "app_label.ModelName" pattern "myapp.User", "auth.*" field Field name "profile", "user" label Message type: "n_plus_one" or "unused_eager_load" "n_plus_one"

docs.title: Wildcard Support
docs.text:
The model field supports fnmatch wildcards:

  • "myapp.*" — matches all models in myapp
  • "*.User" — matches User in any app
  • "*" — matches everything (not recommended)

docs.title: Profiler Whitelisting
docs.text:
When using the Profiler directly, pass whitelist to the constructor:

from django_nplus1 import Profiler

with Profiler(whitelist=[{"model": "User", "field": "profile"}]):
    ...

Note: In the profiler context, model matching uses model.__name__ (no app label prefix), unlike the middleware which uses app_label.ModelName.
